title: Tuchel tells England fans not to lose belief while Ghana fume at penalty decision
description: Thomas Tuchel has dismissed suggestions England were handed a World Cup wake-up call after being held to a goalless draw against Ghana and called on supporters not to lose belief in his side
content:

title: AJ Dybantsa chosen by Washington Wizards with No 1 pick in NBA draft
description: The Washington Wizards selected forward AJ Dybantsa, who led the nation in scoring in his one season at BYU, with the No 1 pick in the NBA draft
content:
AJ Dybantsa is on his way to Washington and ready to start working as soon as he gets there.
That’s not until Wednesday. Tuesday was a night for the NBA‘s No 1 draft pick to party.

title: Injury cloud mars crushing Australia’s Women’s T20 World Cup win over Pakistan
description: Australia have cruised to an easy victory over Pakistan at the Women’s T20 World Cup, but there may be concern over a hand injury to wicketkeeper Beth Mooney
content: Australia effectively locked up a semi-final spot at the Women’s T20 World Cup with the biggest of the four consecutive thrashings they have dealt in this tournament, this time with Pakistan as the unlucky recipient by 113 runs. The result sets up a ... [4049 chars]

title: Want to feel happier at work? Take a five-minute walk
description: Sitting for prolonged periods is associated with health complications – but you can counteract the risks of a sedentary life.
content:
Whether it's responding to emails or taking video calls, so much of office life revolves around being planted on a chair.
But the habit is damaging our health. Prolonged sitting can increase the risk, external of being overweight and developing heart... [1540 chars]

title: Could UK summers get even hotter and are we ready?
description: Based on current trends parts of the UK are set to see 40C summers regularly within a couple of decades.
content:
Much of the UK is facing a record-breaking heatwave, with temperatures potentially reaching 37C in parts of south-east England.
That would smash the previous June high and comes hot on the heels of unprecedented heat in May too.
